name: Jerry Jerome
abstract: Jerry "Buck" Jerome (June 19, 1912 – November 17, 2001) was an American jazz and big band musician, a tenor saxophonist. He played with Glenn Miller, Red Norvo, Benny Goodman and Artie Shaw.

interviews.title: Jerry Jerome interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Johnson Jr.
about_quotes.evidence: The next band was organized by one of Benny Goodman’s saxophone players called Jerry Jerome. ... myself, John Simmons on bass, I can’t think of the piano player’s name, and Arthur Herbert on drums, we were the first band, integrated band to play at Kelly’s Stables on 52nd Street.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Johnson Jr. interviewed by Monk Rowe, Ellenville, New York, June 23, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Conniff
about_quotes.evidence: Georgie Auld was playing tenor, Jerry Jerome was playing tenor you know. It was an all-star band
about_quotes.doc_title: Ray Conniff interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 14, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Powell
about_quotes.evidence: It’s odd that you’d mention that, because I have a tape... on this tape he mentions Jerry Jerome and Jerry Jerome’s affiliation with bebop.
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Powell interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 16, 1999 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Haggart
about_quotes.evidence: Now like Jerry Jerome has never been over there, and there’s a guy who’s made records with Glenn Miller and Benny Goodman, and Artie Shaw.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Haggart interviewed by Michael Woods, Clinton, New York, September 22, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Jo Jones
about_quotes.evidence: Jerry Jerome was playing right across the street from the Reno, directly across the street. He heard that band; he knew that band.

about_quotes.speaker: Jonah Jones
about_quotes.evidence: '57 comes, Irv Jerome, Jerry Jerome's brother, he's got Epic, which was a subsidiary to Victor at this time.
